```json
[
  {
    "idea": "Grocery Store Foot Traffic Collapse Signal",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search volume for 'grocery delivery' and 'food bank near me' through google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in grocery delivery and food bank searches often precede consumer spending contractions and indicate financial strain or shift to online shopping channels.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Consumer discretionary retail faces headwinds when food insecurity searches spike; signals demand destruction in consumer staples and discretionary spending."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When 'grocery delivery' + 'food bank' combined search interest exceeds 75th percentile of 52-week rolling average",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or when combined search interest falls below 50th percentile",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Consumer stress proxies fire monthly; grocery delivery searches spike during economic uncertainty windows which recur seasonally and cyclically."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Rental Car Shortage Volatility Spike",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search volume for 'rental car prices' and 'car rental shortage' through google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in rental car availability/pricing searches correlate with tight vehicle supply, rising leisure travel demand, or fleet disruptions—both inflationary and demand signals.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "High rental car search volume suggests strong leisure travel demand; signals consumer confidence and discretionary spending willingness."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When 'rental car prices' OR 'car rental shortage' search volume exceeds 70th percentile of 26-week rolling baseline",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days or when search volume normalizes below 55th percentile",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal travel demand, holiday planning, and summer leisure travel create recurring rental car search spikes every 3–4 weeks."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Unemployment Claim Anticipation Hedge",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series ICSA (Initial Claims, weekly) through fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When initial jobless claims spike above 2-week moving average by >10%, it signals near-term labor market stress and often precedes equity volatility.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "Claims spikes pressure financial sector credit quality and loan loss provisions; weakness in financial stocks often leads broader selloffs."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When ICSA exceeds its 2-week SMA by ≥10% and closes above prior week's high",
      "exit": "After 5 trading days or when ICSA reverts below 1.05× 2-week SMA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Weekly claims data ensures signal recurrence; seasonal layoffs and economic cycles produce 2–3 spike events per month on average."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Shipping Container Squeeze Play",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "Port of Shanghai weekly container export volumes and backlog through port_container_volume adapter (public shipping indices)",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Elevated container backlogs in major Asian ports signal supply chain tightness and rising freight costs, inflating input prices for manufacturing and retail.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Manufacturing sector faces margin compression from elevated logistics costs; industrials are most exposed to freight cost pass-through friction."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When Shanghai port weekly export backlog exceeds 90th percentile of 52-week rolling history",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or when backlog normalizes below 70th percentile",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal shipping surges (Chinese New Year, holiday inventory builds) and port congestion recur predictably; 2–3 backlog spikes per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Crypto Liquidation Cascade Contagion",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "Yahoo Finance daily Bitcoin and Ethereum volatility (price_only adapter) + Google Trends crypto liquidation mentions through google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",

      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "Tech and fintech firms are exposed to crypto ecosystem; crypto crashes precede tech volatility and venture funding contraction."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When Bitcoin weekly drawdown >15% AND 'crypto liquidation' search interest exceeds 80th percentile of 13-week rolling average",
      "exit": "After 3 trading days or when Bitcoin recovers >8% from entry signal",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Crypto volatility clusters; 15% weekly drawdowns occur 3–4 times per quarter, and liquidation spikes follow predictably within 48 hours."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Temperature Shock Energy Demand Flip",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",

      "source": "Open-Meteo daily average temperature anomalies for US and Europe through weather_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When temperature deviation from 30-year normal exceeds ±2 standard deviations for 3+ consecutive days, it forces emergency energy demand swings and utility margin pressure.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLU",
      "rationale": "Utilities face volume-price mismatches during extreme weather; margin compression and regulatory backlash pressure utility sector equity returns."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When daily US temperature anomaly ≥2σ from historical median AND persists for ≥3 consecutive days",
      "exit": "After 5 trading days or when temperature normalizes to within 1σ",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal heat waves and cold snaps occur predictably; 2–3 extreme temperature events per month during summer/winter transitions."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Seismic Activity Construction Halt Signal",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "USGS earthquake activity (magnitude ≥4.5) by region through earthquake_activity adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true

    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLB",
      "rationale": "Building materials demand falls during seismic event construction halts; project delays inflate materials inventory and reduce near-term demand."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When 2+ earthquakes M≥4.5 occur within same tectonic region within 48 hours",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days or when seismic activity subsides (<1 event per week in region)",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Earthquake clusters are non-uniform but frequent; California/Japan/Chile produce 2–4 M4.5+ clusters per month on average."
  },
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Density Saturation Play",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap daily EV charger counts by state/region through openchargemap adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Rapid week-over-week growth in EV charger deployments (>5% WoW) signals infrastructure buildout completion and maturation—near-term EV demand plateau.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "EV charger saturation signals slowing capex spending on charging; tech companies and EV suppliers face slower order flow."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When weekly EV charger net additions exceed prior 4-week average by ≥25%",
      "exit": "After 8 trading days or when weekly charger additions fall below 2-week moving average",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal infrastructure spending (spring/summer buildout) creates recurring charger installation spikes; 2–4 high-growth weeks per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "News Sentiment Rotation Fade Trade",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "RSS feed (Bloomberg/Reuters/CNBC) daily article count mentioning 'recession' AND 'earnings miss' through rss_count adapter",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When combined recession + earnings miss mentions spike to 90th percentile, sentiment capitulation is near; market contrarian reversal opportunity follows within 3–5 days.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Extreme negative sentiment clustering often marks washout phase; consumer discretionary rebounds as capitulation exhausts."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When combined daily RSS count (recession + earnings miss) exceeds 85th percentile of 52-week rolling distribution",
      "exit": "After 4 trading days or when RSS count reverts to 50th percentile",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Earnings seasons and recession narratives cycle; 2–3 sentiment spikes per month during earnings windows and Fed announcement clusters."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Healthcare Cost Shock Google Search Spike",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search volume for 'medical debt' and 'prescription drug cost' through google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true

    "behavior": "Spikes in healthcare cost-related searches signal consumer financial strain and pressure on healthcare utilization; precedes healthcare sector demand pullback.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLV",
      "rationale": "Rising search interest in medical debt and prescription costs signals demand elasticity; healthcare equity valuations compress on utilization concerns."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When 'medical debt' + 'prescription drug cost' combined search interest exceeds 72nd percentile of 26-week rolling baseline",
      "exit": "After 6 trading days or when combined search interest falls below 50th percentile",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Insurance deductible resets (Jan), drug price negotiations, and benefits season (Nov) create 2–3 cost-shock search spikes per quarter."
  }
```
